Sales were still better than expected.

LULU.headline: Lululemon ends proxy battle with founder Chip Wilson
LULU.summary: STORY: Lululemon Athletica has ended its boardroom battle with founder Chip Wilson.The company agreed to give Wilson, who was CEO until 2005, two board picks in exchange for his pledge to stay quiet for around 18 months.The deal ends a messy proxy fight that Wilson started late last year, when he nominated three board directors and accused the company of having lost its "cool" factor.Lululemon in turn said Wilson had "outdated perspectives" about how to position the athleisure brand as it dealt with a drop in North American sales and a falling stock price due to increased competition.Lululemon said Wilson, who left the board in 2015 and owns 8.7% of the company, agreed to a contractual clause that caps his stake.Shares rose as much as almost seven percent in Wednesday morning trading after the company confirmed the story Reuters reported Tuesday.

LULU.headline: Lululemon Board Shake-Up; Bath and Body Works Overhaul | Stock Movers

The company will name two of Wilson's nominees to the board in Laura Gentile, a former ESPN executive, and Marc Maurer, the previous co-chief executive officer of sneaker maker On. A third new director will be announced by October 1. - Bath and Body Works (BBWI) shares are higher after it reported lower first quarter sales and says it's overhauling its strategy to return to growth. Sales were still better than expected.

NCLH.summary: Oil prices fell back below $90 a barrel on Thursday morning on a report that U.S. and Iranian negotiators had agreed on a framework for a 60-day ceasefire extension after renewed Persian Gulf attacks had threatened the fragile truce.  Axios said that President Donald Trump had not yet given his approval, but the S&P 500 climbed, aiming to extend its six-session winning streak.  As hopes rise that a resolution of the U.S.-Iran stalemate will reopen the Strait of Hormuz, the clock is ticking to some near-term bad scenarios for both Iran and the global economy.

On a $3 million entry-level luxury home, that's roughly $198,000 more cash at closing, driven by AI workers liquidating equity and putting it straight into real estate.

According to Zillow's latest rental report, concessions — incentives such as free rent, waived fees and discounted move-in costs — are showing up on nearly 40% of listings. For renters, that can translate into meaningful savings both upfront and over the course of a lease.

Z.headline: Zillow: Excellent Value As Rentals Drive Sustained Growth
Z.summary: Zillow (Z) remains a buy, despite a ~50% YTD decline, due to sustainable growth catalysts and product innovation.

Z.headline: Top Economist Sounds Alarm On 'Extremely Unaffordable' US Housing Market— 'Better Late Than…'
Z.summary: Mohamed El-Erian warns U.S. housing remains extremely unaffordable as buyers spend 42% of income on homes.

ZBAO.summary: Before the opening bell on Wednesday, let's take a glimpse of the US markets and explore the top gainers and losers in today's pre-market session.

ZBAO.summary: Semtech Corp (SMTC) reported strong Q1 financial results, beating analysts' estimates. Shares rose 7% in pre-market trading as Q2 guidance exceeded expectations.

ZBH.headline: Citigroup Maintains Neutral on Zimmer Biomet Holdings, Lowers Price Target to $93
ZBH.summary: Citigroup  analyst Joanne Wuensch   maintains Zimmer Biomet Holdings (NYSE:ZBH) with a Neutral and lowers the price target from $95 to $93.

ZBH.headline: Zimmer (ZBH) Up 3.1% Since Last Earnings Report: Can It Continue?
ZBH.summary: Zimmer (ZBH) reported earnings 30 days ago. What's next for the stock? We take a look at earnings estimates for some clues.

ZBH.headline: 3 Healthcare Stocks That Fall Short
ZBH.summary: Healthcare companies are pushing the status quo by innovating in areas like drug development and digital health. But speed bumps such as inventory destocking have persisted in the wake of COVID-19, limiting growth. This has capped returns as the industry’s six-month gain of 1.2% has lagged the S&P 500’s 9.8% climb.

ZBH.headline: THINK Surgical Announces the TMINI® Miniature Robotic System 5,000th Procedure Milestone
ZBH.summary: THINK Surgical, Inc., an innovator in the field of orthopedic surgical robots, today announces that its TMINI® Miniature Robotic System has been used in its 5,000th total knee (TKA) procedure. The 5,000th case was recently completed by Dr. John Tiberi of Cedars-Sinai Orthopedics in Los Angeles, CA.

ZBH.headline: 3 Reasons to Sell ZBH and 1 Stock to Buy Instead
ZBH.summary: Over the past six months, Zimmer Biomet’s shares (currently trading at $85.45) have posted a disappointing 12.4% loss, well below the S&P 500’s 9.1% gain. This might have investors contemplating their next move.

ZEPP.headline: Verra Mobility, Zscaler, Box And Other Big Stocks Moving Lower In Wednesday's Pre-Market Session
ZEPP.summary: Stock futures rise on Wednesday, with Verra Mobility's shares plunging 45% after Avis Budget Group terminated its agreement with the company. Other stocks moving lower in pre-market trading include Zscaler, Brain, LG Display, Zepp Health, Globalfoundries, Posco Holdings, Insulet, Palisade Bio, Satellogic, and Box.

ZION.headline: ZIONS BANCORPORATION TO PRESENT AT THE MORGAN STANLEY US FINANCIALS CONFERENCE
ZION.summary: Scott McLean, President and COO of Zions Bancorporation, N.A. (NASDAQ: ZION), will present at the Morgan Stanley US Financials Conference on Tuesday, June 9th at 4:45 pm Eastern. An audio webcast of the session may be accessed on the Zions Bancorporation website, www.zionsbancorporation.com. A replay will also be made available following the event.
